text: A production model is usually deployed behind an API gateway that performs {{c1::routing}}, {{c2::rate limiting}}, and {{c3::request logging}}.
explanation: Deployment is not only about serving a model; it also requires secure traffic control, quotas, and observability.
usage: Core pattern for enterprise AI systems and internal LLM applications.
spanish: Un modelo de producción suele desplegarse detrás de una pasarela API que realiza enrutamiento, limitación de velocidad y registro de solicitudes.

text: Observability in ML systems means tracking {{c1::latency}}, {{c2::cost}}, and {{c3::drift}} across model versions and user segments.
explanation: A model can work technically and still fail in production if teams cannot see quality degradation or rising costs.
usage: Essential for model monitoring dashboards and incident response.
spanish: La observabilidad en sistemas de ML significa rastrear latencia, costo y deriva entre versiones de modelos y segmentos de usuarios.

text: Data drift occurs when the distribution of incoming features changes, while concept drift happens when the target relationship itself {{c1::changes}}.
explanation: Drift detection is critical because a model trained on old patterns may become unreliable in a new context.
usage: Used in monitoring pipelines for finance, fraud, and recommendation systems.
spanish: La deriva de datos ocurre cuando cambia la distribución de las características, mientras que la deriva conceptual ocurre cuando cambia la relación con el objetivo.

text: Prompt caching stores reusable prefixes so the system can avoid recomputing the same {{c1::context}} for repeated requests.
explanation: This reduces latency and token cost for large-system prompts and multi-turn conversations.
usage: Very useful for assistants, copilots, and enterprise knowledge bots.
spanish: El almacenamiento en caché de prompts guarda prefijos reutilizables para evitar recomputar el mismo contexto en solicitudes repetidas.

text: A robust deployment pipeline includes a {{c1::rollback strategy}} so teams can revert to a previous model version quickly when issues appear.
explanation: Rollback is a crucial operational safeguard for AI systems because failures are often discovered only after release.
usage: Common in continuous delivery pipelines and model registries.
spanish: Un pipeline de despliegue robusto incluye una estrategia de reversión para restaurar rápidamente una versión anterior del modelo si aparecen problemas.
